title: Adesanya walloped by Pyfer in 2nd-round finish

title: Braves' Smith hits walk-off days after mom's death
content: Making his Braves debut, Dominic Smith said he "got choked up" thinking about his late mother as he celebrated his walk-off grand slam in Saturday's 6-2 win over the Royals.

title: Illinois' Underwood fulfills Final Four 'dream'
content: Keaton Wagler scored 25 points as Illinois beat Iowa on Saturday, sending the program to the Final Four for the first time in 21 years.

title: USMNT routed by Belgium in blow to WCup prep
content: The USMNT suffered a setback in its preparations for the World Cup as Belgium ran rampant in the second half to emerge with a 5-2 win at Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Saturday.

title: Easily out, Dodgers' Freeman tagged out with hug
content: With nowhere to run on the basepaths, the Dodgers' Freddie Freeman walked toward a smiling Ketel Marte, who tagged him out with a hug to end the second inning of Saturday night's game.

title: UFC fighter called back to cage after scorecard flub
content: Tyrell Fortune had to return to the Octagon on Saturday after UFC cage announcer Bruce Buffer mistakenly announced his opponent Marcin Tybura as the winner.

title: Mayweather pumps brakes on Pacquiao rematch
content: Floyd Mayweather cast some doubt on details of his pending fight with Manny Pacquiao, saying the location has not be set and that the bout will actually just be an exhibition.

content: It was a difficult game for the U.S. against Belgium, with Tim Weah struggling to make a positive impact.

content: The CBA permanently and positively changes the WNBA's landscape. These are the wins, biggest strides and areas of compromise.

content: Women's Super League champions-elect Manchester City thrashed second-place Man United to go 11 points clear and show why they are the best around.

title: Lehigh Valley IronPigs poke fun at Zack Wheeler's rib removal in rehab promo
content: The Phillies' ace had a rib removed as part of a surgery to remove a blood clot -- and kept it in his closet.

content: Rising crude oil and natural gas costs are increasing construction expenses and disrupting material supply, creating uncertainty for developers and homebuyers

content: Virat Kohli's 38-ball 69, studded with five sixes and as many fours, powered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a comfortable six-wicket win over Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL season opener on March 28

title: High up, and soaring: Chennai’s rental market
content: From core city neighbourhoods to fast-growing suburbs, Chennai’s rental market is witnessing a sharp upswing. Nearly 49% of the households in the city still depend on rental accommodation, data show. More often than not, tenants are forced to compromise on space and budget. This report presents a list of emerging rental trends

title: 2 Indian, 1 Pakistani ships cross Strait of Hormuz
content: Two other LPG carriers belonging to the state-owned SCI had crossed the strait and have discharged their cargo in India.

title: 40 pilgrims injured as bus rams into tree in Uttar Pradesh's Mau
content: The accident occurred around 1.30 a.m. on the Ratanpura-Mubarakpur road under the Haldharpur police station area when the driver lost control of the bus and rammed it into a roadside tree, police said.

title: West Asia crisis: Rajnath Singh calls for swift decisions, long-term readiness
content: Mr. Singh made the remarks while chairing the first meeting of the Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM) set up to monitor the situation in West Asia

title: CRPF officer who lost leg in line of duty battling pain, stagnant career
content: Ajay Malik, an Assistant Commandant with CRPF, lost his leg after he stepped on an IED in Jharkhand forest; retired CAPF personnel say officers face career stagnation though they work under grave conditions

content: Why was the Bill introduced in the Rajya Sabha? Why is there opposition to it? What is the government’s justification?

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: Inflation, a major talking point ahead of polls
content: The spike in food prices has emerged as a major concern for the public. A long-standing welfare architecture in Kerala has attempted to cushion the effects of inflation, especially for the vulnerable. However, a few experts have raised doubts about the effectiveness of the State government’s market intervention measures

title: NIMHANS study flags rising parental concern over children’s screen time
content: ‘Parents are increasingly reporting difficulties in regulating their children’s technology use, especially when it begins to interfere with sleep, studies and daily routines’

content: Responding to questions posed by Kodikunnil Suresh and Adoor Prakash in the Lok Sabha, Union Minister of Health said “the proposal for establishment of AIIMS in Kerala has not been approved” and provided no concrete timeline for future approval

title: What did the Supreme Court rule on Scheduled Castes and religion?
content: What did the Supreme Court say about Scheduled Caste status for those not belonging to Hinduism, Sikhism, or Buddhism? Can SC status be restored through reconversion? What is the Centre’s position on SC status for Dalit Muslims and Christians? What about Scheduled Tribes?

title: News in Frames: Licensed to bloom
content: Farming communities in Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan cultivate opium, a practice passed down for generations and tied to tradition and prestige alike

title: Bengaluru Metro services disrupted on Purple Line for maintenance
content: The temporary halt disrupted early morning commuters travelling along the busy Purple Line corridor, as the maintenance announcement came only on Sunday morning when the work began, leaving passengers with no time to alter their plans or arrange alternative modes of transport.

title: Prevent allotment of reserved election symbols of one party to a member of another party, urges a PIL plea before Madras High Court
content: Litigant says, it has become a common practice among members of political parties to contest in reserved symbols of alliance partners

title: PM Modi speaks to Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia; discusses West Asia situation
content: In his telephonic conversation with the Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia, the Prime Minister also reiterated India's condemnation of attacks on regional energy infrastructure

content: The BJP leader, who served as Governor of Mizoram and Goa, talks about his decision not to contest the Assembly polls from Chengannur, the BJP-led NDA’s chances, and the focused attacks on the BJP by the LDF and the UDF

title: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy conveys his resolve to go ahead with Musi development
content: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy said a mosque is scheduled to be constructed at Charminar, besides Gurdwara at Sikh Chawni in Gowliguda and church on the Nagole-Uppal stretch

content: The maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 38°C and 25°C respectively,” according to IMD bulletin

content: Expressing his apprehension, Mr. Mohan added that in days to come or in near future, the ruling parties will not get any benefit from the decision of Nitish Kumar leaving Bihar politics and moving to Rajya Sabha

title: Great Indian Bustard chick born in Gujarat after a decade through jumpstart approach: Environment Minister
content: The field monitoring team is observing the young chick being reared by its foster mother in its natural habitat after a significant achievement in the recovery of the critically endangered species

title: Chhattisgarh Govt cracks down on LPG cylinder hoarding, 97 FIRs registered
content: A comprehensive review of the availability and supply of petroleum products, LPG cylinders, fertilisers and other essential commodities was undertaken during a high-level review meeting chaired by Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai

content: India is now among the world’s largest LPG consumers, with annual demand touching nearly 31 million tonnes, but has storage capacity sufficient for barely three to four weeks

content: O. Panneerselvam gets Bodinayakanur; his supporters R. Vaithilingam and Manoj Pandian have been fielded in Orathanadu and Alangulam

content: In 1994, the Indian Air Force made him an Honorary Air Commodore in recognition of the over 5,000 hours of flying that he had put in.

title: Case Against Kerala Leader For Offering UAE Trip To Booth Workers
content: Authorities have recovered video footage of the convention in which the IUML leader allegedly made the offer.

content: The elderly couple argued that their daughter-in-law has sufficient independent income and has also received service benefits after their son's death.

content: The war in the Middle East began when the US and Israel conducted strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and other senior leaders.

content: US Israel Iran War News LIVE Updates: The United States and Israel continue to strike Iran, whose retaliatory attacks have targeted Israel and neighboring Gulf Arab states. More than 3,000 people have been killed.

content: "The freedom of one person ceases where the statutory right of another person starts," the court said in a March 20 ruling

content: The Bab el-Mandeb strait at the southern tip of the Red Sea is now emerging as a second front in what experts describe as the most severe disruption to global energy markets in modern history.

content: The three journalists were identified as Al Mayadeen's Fatima Ftouni, her brother and colleague Mohammed, and Al-Manar's Ali Shaib.

title: Millions Join 'No Kings' Rallies Across US, Europe To Protest Against Trump
content: People rallied from New York City, with almost 8.5 million residents in a solidly blue state, to Driggs, a town of fewer than 2,000 people in eastern Idaho, a state Trump carried with 66% of the vote in 2024.

title: Bengaluru Man Slits Mother, Sister, Nephew's Throats Then Attempts To Kill Self, 2 Dead
content: The victims have been identified as Asha (55) and her daughter Varshitha (34), who died on the spot. Varshitha's brother Mohan Gowda (32) and her 11yearold son Mayank sustained severe injuries

title: Raids Near Hormuz, Seizing Kharg: US Prepares For Weeks Of Ground Ops In Iran
content: Whether Donald Trump would approve any of those plans remains uncertain, the Washington Post reported.

title: Over 4,00,000 Kitkat Bars Stolen From Cargo Enroute Poland From Italy
content: The shipment of the crunchy bars, made of waffles covered with chocolate, disappeared last week while en route between production and distribution locations.

title: Ex-Agniveers To Get 20% Delhi Police Constable Posts, Physical Test Not Needed
content: Ex-Agniveers will be exempted from the Physical Efficiency Test (PET), one of the most competitive stages of the recruitment process.

title: Israel Kills 3 Journalists in Lebanon Strike, Claims 1 Was Hezbollah Member
content: Ali Shoeib of Hezbollah's Al Manar channel and Fatima Ftouni of Al Mayadeen, seen as close to the Iran-backed movement, were killed in Jezzine, alongside Ftouni's brother, a cameraman.

title: Great Indian Bustard Chick Born In Kutch After 10 Years
content: The number of birds at the breeding centres established at Sam and Ramdevara in Rajasthan has now increased to 73.

title: Iran Threatens To Hit US University Campuses In Middle East
content: Several US universities have campuses scattered throughout the Gulf region, such as Texas A&M University in Qatar and New York University in the United Arab Emirates.

title: Tiger poisoned to death near Satpura Reserve; five arrested
content: According to officials, the decomposed body of the female tiger was found buried outside the Reserve’s boundary in the Sangakheda range of the West Chhindwara Division after which the forest officials swung into action.

title: J&K House condemns attack on Abdullah; Minister Itoo questions silence of police, Home dept.
content: J&K Peoples Conference legislator Sajad Lone said the governments in J&K “have been using security cover for political ends”

title: T.N. Assembly election: Premallatha to contest in Vriddhachalam; Virudhunagar allotted to Vijayaprabakaran
content: The party was allotted 10 Assembly constituencies and a Rajya Sabha seat by the DMK. The party’s treasurer L.K. Sudhish was elected as a Rajya Sabha Member recently

title: T.N. Assembly election: DMK candidate list includes 60 fresh faces and 4 Mayors
content: The list also includes 19 women candidates. Of the 164 nominees, 15 are doctors, 17 engineers, 29 advocates, and seven hold Ph.Ds

title: BIMS’ golden jubilee fete ends, alumni association takes shape
content: Past students felicitate D. Anand, senior professor, who is retiring after an impressive journey in the Department of Studies in Business Administration

title: When defeat becomes apparent person loses control over words, says Samajwadi Party on PM Modi’s ‘ATM for loot’ jibe
content: Opposition party states Noida evolved as an economic engine because of planned industrial clusters, affordable housing, metro connectivity, and opportunities for small and medium enterprises initiated by previous SP government

title: 'Petrol, diesel crisis developing worldwide': PM Modi cautions during Mann ki Baat
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi warned of a global petrol and diesel shortage due to ongoing conflicts impacting vital energy regions. Speaking on Mann Ki Baat, he highlighted how recent wars, particularly in the Gulf, are disrupting energy supplies.  He expressed gratitude to Gulf nations for assisting over a crore Indians residing there amidst the escalating tensions.

title: ‘Put down the crown, clown’: Thousands rally against Trump in 'No Kings' protests
content: Thousands of people joined “No Kings” protests across the United States and parts of Europe, opposing the war in Iran and the policies of Donald Trump. Minnesota became the focal point, with a major rally in St Paul led by Bruce Springsteen. While most protests were peaceful, some clashes were reported, and Republican leaders dismissed the demonstrations as politically motivated.

title: Bengaluru horror: Man slits throats of mother, sister, nephew, then attempts suicide
content: A family of four attempted suicide due to mounting debts, resulting in two deaths and two critically injured. Preliminary investigations suggest financial distress from a chit fund business and a daughter's medical treatment led to the tragedy. The family recorded a video before the incident, citing pressure from lenders.

title: Google ex-CEO Eric Schmidt says coding is no longer a key skill
content: 0Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt says the best programmers no longer write code—they write specs, set evaluation criteria, and let AI work overnight. Schmidt described a startup programmer whose AI finishes by 4 AM what would have taken him six months and 10 engineers at Google. He argued top programmers will become more valuable, not less, and that AI's biggest impact will be automating costly corporate operations like billing, accounting, and logistics.

title: Virat Kohli surpasses Pakistan batter to become Asia’s top T20 run-scorer
content: Virat Kohli has etched his name in T20 history, becoming the Asian batter with the most runs, surpassing Shoaib Malik.  Achieving this feat in the IPL opener, Kohli's unbeaten 69 guided RCB to victory. He attributed his sustained performance to careful workload management and a commitment to team success, highlighting his enduring impact on the game.

title: US proposes wage increase for H-1B, H-1B1 and E-3 visas
content: The Trump administration is proposing a significant hike in prevailing wages for H-1B and employment-based visa holders, aiming to increase minimum salaries by 21-33%. This move by the Department of Labor seeks to protect American jobs and wages by ensuring foreign workers are paid market rates, curbing potential displacement of U.S. workers by lower-paid foreign labor.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ makes over Rs 1,200 crore worldwide in 10 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is performing strongly at the box office, crossing Rs 1,200 crore worldwide after a 10-day run. The action-thriller saw a significant surge on its second Saturday, contributing to its impressive domestic and global collections. The film continues the story of an undercover agent battling terrorism.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: DMK releases list of 164 candidates, fields more than 60 new faces
content: In a bold move ahead of the elections, DMK has rolled out its roster of 164 candidates, blending experienced stalwarts with more than 60 fresh entrants eager to make their mark. Chief Minister M.K. Stalin championed the 'mega mega alliance' during the campaign kickoff, setting sights on the western region.

title: 'Virat Kohli was over': Mohammad Kaif's post after RCB win goes viral
content: Two years after retiring from T20Is, Virat Kohli continues to shine, scoring an unbeaten 69 to guide Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a big win over Sunrisers Hyderabad. Mohammad Kaif praised his form. Kohli also broke records, becoming the first to 4000 IPL chase runs, as RCB sealed a dominant victory.

title: 'Evil', 'liar': Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's war against Altman & OpenAI gets personal
content: A decade-old rift between Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and OpenAI's Sam Altman has resurfaced amid public criticism and policy disputes over military AI.  Their disagreement, reportedly starting in a San Francisco house, escalated over AI development transparency and research credit, leading to Amodei's departure to found Anthropic.  Recent exchanges highlight their ongoing friction.

title: ‘Targets around globe’: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un oversees high-thrust solid-fuel rocket engine test
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw a test of a high-thrust solid-fuel rocket engine as part of the country’s defence plan, according to Korean Central News Agency. The development highlights efforts to strengthen missile capabilities, with analysts saying the improved engine could support long-range weapons, including intercontinental ballistic missiles, and enhance strike capacity.

title: Saudi Arabia pumps 7 million bpd via east-west pipeline amid Hormuz disruption
content: Saudi Arabia has fully operationalized its East-West pipeline, moving 7 million barrels of oil daily to the Red Sea port of Yanbu. This crucial alternative route aims to maintain export supplies following disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz. While not fully replacing Hormuz volumes, the pipeline's operation is mitigating significant price increases amid escalating regional tensions.

title: IPL: Can CSK learn to live without Dhoni? Legend set to miss up to six games
content: MS Dhoni's 'final' IPL season faces an early setback with a calf strain, potentially sidelining him for three weeks and at least six matches. This injury forces CSK to rely on a new leadership group and opens opportunities for younger players in the lower order, while Sanju Samson will handle wicket-keeping duties.

title: From office to dark streets: How oil crunch is reshaping daily life in different nations
content: Global economies reel as Middle East conflict escalates, disrupting oil supplies via the Strait of Hormuz. Nations like Vietnam and India are cutting fuel taxes, while Japan and Germany consider increased coal use. Venezuela faces power cuts, and Pakistan grapples with soaring jet fuel prices. Sri Lanka implements strict fuel rationing, highlighting widespread energy challenges.

title: BJP MLA Nabin Kumar Jain booked for firing blanks during Ram Navami procession in Odisha
content: BJP MLA Nabin Kumar Jain faces charges for firing blanks from his licensed pistol during a Ram Navami procession in Titlagarh. Police registered a case under the BNS and Arms Act after a video surfaced showing the legislator firing three rounds. His personal security officer was suspended for allegedly assisting him.

title: RCB rewrite record books with fastest 200+ chase in IPL history
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru achieved the IPL's fastest 200-plus chase, defeating Sunrisers Hyderabad by six wickets. Chasing 202, RCB reached the target in just 15.4 overs, powered by a rapid 101-run partnership between Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal. Padikkal's explosive 61 off 26 balls set the tone for the record-breaking victory.

title: Full scale invasion or special ops? Inside Pentagon plan for US boots in Iran
content: The US military is developing plans for potential ground operations in Iran, ranging from targeted raids to limited troop deployments, as a contingency measure. While the White House maintains no decision has been made, the Pentagon is preparing options to neutralize specific threats, reflecting a widening gap between readiness and cautious public messaging.

title: 8th rape case against Ashok Kharat: Two more FIRs against bogus astrologer
content: Nashik police have registered two new cases against astrologer Ashok Kharat, bringing the total to ten. Allegations include repeated sexual assaults on a woman and cheating a trader out of Rs 2.6 lakh. The woman claims Kharat exploited her marital issues, while the trader was defrauded with fake gemstones.

title: Piyush Mishra praises ‘Dhurandhar 2’: ‘It is cinema actually’
content: At the Delhi International Film Festival, esteemed actor Piyush Mishra celebrated Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2', referring to it as 'true cinema' and dismissing any notions of propaganda surrounding it. He commended its remarkable screenplay. Additionally, notable figures in the industry such as Rajinikanth, Allu Arjun, and Alia Bhatt have also expressed their admiration for the film's direction and standout performances.

title: ‘No words for Virat Kohli’: Rajat Patidar hails RCB star after emphatic win
content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru launched their IPL 2026 title defence with a commanding six-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad. Virat Kohli's unbeaten 69 was the cornerstone of the chase, with skipper Rajat Patidar hailing him as the team's backbone. Debutant Jacob Duffy's early wickets also proved crucial in securing the win.

title: 1 dead, 2 injured after ceiling collapses at Tamil Nadu's Samayapuram Temple
content: A devotee died and two others were injured when a portion of the ceiling collapsed at the Samayapuram Mariamman Temple in Trichy. The incident occurred in the Sannathi Street mandapam where devotees were resting. Police have cordoned off the area and are investigating the mishap at the prominent Shakti shrine.

title: Love, lies & trading trap: Bengaluru techie falls for marriage offer, loses 18.7L
content: A Bengaluru software engineer was defrauded of Rs 18.7 lakh by a woman he met on a dating app.  Initially building trust with promises of a serious relationship and financial support, she lured him into online trading.  Despite early profits, he was eventually unable to withdraw funds, leading to his realization of the scam.

title: Man arrested after car hits pedestrians in UK's Derby, several injured —what we know so far
content: A man has been arrested after a car hit multiple pedestrians in Derby city centre, leaving several people injured, some seriously. Derbyshire Constabulary said the incident involved a black Suzuki Swift and took place on Friar Gate. Police have ruled out any ongoing risk but continue to investigate, while local MP Catherine Atkinson said she was “deeply shocked” by the incident.

title: Watch: Virat Kohli blows flying kiss to Anushka Sharma after RCB’s dominant win
content: Virat Kohli's unbeaten 69 powered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL 2026 opener.  Kohli's stellar performance, coupled with a romantic gesture towards wife Anushka Sharma, captivated fans. Devdutt Padikkal's quickfire 61 also proved crucial in the successful chase, while Ishan Kishan's 80 wasn't enough for SRH.

title: 4 injured after jumping from first floor to escape blaze in Delhi’s Bhajanpura
content: Four people were injured after jumping from a house in Bhajanpura to escape a fire that started in parked two-wheelers. The blaze, which began early Sunday morning, required 11 fire tenders to control. In separate incidents, fires were also reported at a factory in Bawana and the Hard Rock Cafe in Connaught Place, with no casualties in the latter.

title: 'Bengalis led war for Independence. Who were the Gujaratis?': MP Mahua Moitra
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah released the BJP's "chargesheet" against the Mamata Banerjee government, alleging corruption and violence. The Trinamool Congress hit back with its own "chargesheet," accusing the BJP of insulting Bengalis and withholding funds. Both parties escalated rhetoric ahead of the upcoming elections.

title: Former sarpanch dies waiting 4 hours for LPG in 41°C heat in Maharashtra
content: A former sarpanch died while waiting for an LPG cylinder in Akola's sweltering heat. Shankarrao Sirsat collapsed after hours in the queue. The incident has sparked anger and questions about the administration's preparedness for essential supply. Citizens are demanding regular LPG availability. Political leaders have criticized the government's arrangements.

title: 15 lakh Bengal voters in limbo due to missing e-signatures
content: EC officials said 60 lakh voters were initially marked for scrutiny after a special intensive revision (SIR) of rolls, with over 700 judicial officers assigned to review them. By March 23 evening, around 29 lakh cases had been cleared. Bengal CEO Manoj Agarwal said names carrying e-signatures till 5pm would be published in the first supplementary list. The list released around midnight carried about 10 lakh names.

title: Tiger Woods DUI arrest sparks tension, Vanessa Trump reacts
content: Tiger Woods is dealing with both legal trouble and personal pressure after a DUI arrest report by the Daily Mail. The report claims Vanessa Trump reacted strongly and asked him to fix his situation. Woods is said to be embarrassed and considering treatment. No official statement has been made, and both his case and relationship remain uncertain.

title: Dallas Cowboys eye Eagles legend’s son in trade twist
content: Josiah Trotter’s pre-draft workout with the Dallas Cowboys highlights growing interest in a linebacker who has quickly built a strong resume despite early setbacks. With deep family ties to the Eagles and a skill set suited to Dallas’ evolving defense, Trotter represents both a practical solution and a compelling storyline. As the Cowboys reassess a thin linebacker unit, his name is firmly in the mix.

title: Baker Mayfield Bucs linked to $118M Commanders linebacker
content: Following Lavonte David’s retirement annoucement, The Athletic suggests the Tampa Bay Buccaneers target veteran linebacker Bobby Wagner to fill the legendary gap. Wagner is a Super Bowl champion with extensive accolades to his name. He has a high football IQ. He possesses elite run-stopping and pass-rushing abilities. This could potentially fit into Tampa Bay’s needs.

title: PM Modi backs ethanol blending as key to cutting crude imports
content: “Farmers’ efforts in this have given a lot of relief to the country at this time of global crisis,” he said. Ethanol production, he added, has not only benefited the nation but also farmers, with approximately Rs 1.5 lakh crore worth of foreign exchange being saved. Jewar is a gateway to the sugarcane belts of western UP. Sugarcane feedstock is a key source of ethanol.

title: Fact check: Bradley Dack, Olivia Attwood cheating claims and marriage truth?
content: Bradley Dack is facing multiple cheating claims as several women speak out, according to The Sun. At the same time, Daily Mail reports reveal he and Olivia Attwood were never legally married despite their 2023 ceremony. The couple had planned to register later, but Olivia backed out after discovering issues, leading to their split earlier this year.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce NDA speculation takes dramatic twist
content: Rumors about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce enforcing strict NDAs at their wedding spread quickly after Graham Norton joked about signing them. Fans and media took it seriously, fueling speculation about a secretive ceremony. Norton later clarified it was a joke that got misreported. There is no confirmed NDA, and the couple has not shared any official wedding details yet.

title: Fact check: Did Cardi B really owe Stefon Diggs $1M?
content: Rumors linking Cardi B and Stefon Diggs went viral after a social media post claimed a fallout and a $1 million dispute. The speculation grew when an alleged deleted Instagram story surfaced. However, there is no verified proof supporting these claims. Neither party has responded publicly, and no credible reports confirm the narrative, making the controversy largely driven by unverified online speculation.

title: IPL 2026’s first controversy! Klaasen fumes over ‘debatable’ boundary catch
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured a dominant win over Sunrisers Hyderabad, but a controversial catch by Phil Salt, which led to Heinrich Klaasen's dismissal, sparked debate. Despite the umpire's decision, replays suggested Salt might have touched the boundary sponge. Virat Kohli's unbeaten 69 then powered RCB's successful chase of 202.

title: Delhi shocker: Gang lures man using dating app, robs him of Rs 7 lakh
content: A man was lured to a meeting via a dating app in Nehru Place, only to be abducted, assaulted, and robbed of ₹7 lakh. Five individuals, including a woman, have been apprehended by Delhi Police following a thorough investigation involving CCTV analysis and digital tracking. The victim was forced to make UPI transfers before being abandoned.

title: Luka Doncic faces setback as Lakers star gets bad news after split
content: Luka Doncic’s 16th technical foul has finally resulted in a one-game suspension, with the NBA declining to rescind the call this time. The Lakers star will miss a game against the Wizards but remains in strong form on the court. However, he now faces the risk of further suspensions if his technical count rises again. Off the court, his ongoing breakup and legal dispute add another layer of pressure during a crucial stretch of the season.

title: IPL 2026: Virat Kohli scripts history, becomes the first player to…
content: Virat Kohli etched his name in IPL history, playing his 19th consecutive season for Royal Challengers Bengaluru.  He anchored RCB's chase with an unbeaten 69, guiding them to a six-wicket victory over Sunrisers Hyderabad.  Devdutt Padikkal's explosive 61 set the stage for the dominant win, while Ishan Kishan's 80 was the highlight for SRH.

title: Bengaluru professor who called student 'terrorist' 13 times suspended
content: In a shocking turn of events, a professor from PES University has been suspended following the release of a video where he repeatedly referred to a minority student as a 'terrorist' during a lecture. The incident, which took place on March 24, has prompted the police to initiate a suo motu criminal case.

content: the fifth installment of our premier league MVP rankings aims at tackling a mighty question: which players rank as the most important at the division's top clubs? we used a simple combination of ability and tactical factors to assess how crucial the players were to their teams .

title: Takeaways from every Friday game
content: the 2026 season has arrived, and as of Friday night, all 30 teams have taken the field . from the Yankees' opening night win over the Giants to the blue Jays' walk-off victory over the A's . the good: Kazuma Okamoto went 2-for-3 with a walk .

title: Men's tournament: Arizona the title favorite as Duke's odds improve to second
content: Duke has claimed its best odds to win the national championship since the tournament began . a solid win over St. John's in the regional semifinals showed +255 to be second on the odds board just behind Arizona (+245) . both teams will be sizable underdogs to the blue Devils and Wolverines in the finals .
